summary: Backend-only testing of the n8n Webhook Chat proxy (FastAPI + MongoDB). Wrote pytest suite covering all 14 requested behaviours across health, session CRUD, chat proxy, webhook ping and cleanup. 18/18 tests passed on the external REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py uses deprecated FastAPI @app.on_event('shutdown') hook - consider migrating to lifespan context manager (non-blocking).

extract_reply() picks only first matched key from a fixed list - if a user's n8n workflow returns a JSON with a nested string (e.g. {data:{output:'...'}}), it will fall through to stringified JSON. Acceptable per spec but worth documenting.
CORS is wide open via '*' which is fine for this public proxy use-case but flag for production hardening.
No explicit validation that message content is non-empty in /api/chat - allows empty user message to be persisted and proxied. Minor.
test_credentials: N/A - no auth in this app
seed_data_creation: Tests create ephemeral sessions with 'TEST_' titles and clean them up in TestDelete at end of run; also uses public postman-echo and httpbin for the external webhook.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend is fully green. Test file /app/backend/tests/test_webhook_chat.py can be re-run as a regression suite. It depends on reachability of postman-echo.com and httpbin.org. For unreachable-URL tests it uses a .invalid TLD which resolves to DNS failure - does NOT need network isolation. If frontend needs testing next, focus on: (a) localStorage webhook URL persistence, (b) sidebar session list re-fetch after title auto-rename, (c) error bubble styling when assistant_message.error=true, (d) dark theme contrast.
